metaTitle: How Often Should a Boiler Be Serviced? | London and Sussex Guide
metaDescription: How often should a boiler be serviced? Learn the usual service interval, why annual servicing matters and when to book sooner.
h1: How Often Should a Boiler Be Serviced?
intro: Most boilers should be serviced every year. An annual service helps catch wear, safety issues and efficiency problems before they turn into a breakdown or a bigger repair bill.

sections.heading: Why annual servicing is the usual standard
sections.body: A yearly service gives the boiler a regular safety and performance check. That interval is frequent enough to spot wear and changes in system behaviour without leaving long gaps.

sections.heading: When you should book sooner than the annual date
sections.body: Book earlier if the boiler has started making noise, pressure keeps dropping, the hot water is inconsistent or the appliance is showing new fault codes.

sections.heading: What servicing helps prevent
sections.body: Servicing can reduce the chance of avoidable breakdowns by identifying leaks, pressure issues, combustion concerns and worn components before they worsen.
